steps.title: Identify what each text addresses
steps.content: The key signal is the number of possible causes examined. Text 1 focuses on ONE cause (asteroid impact) and the features that support it. Text 2 evaluates TWO alternative causes (carbonate dissolution and subsurface salt withdrawal) to determine whether they fit Nadir.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Option C identifies the core difference: Text 1 works with one explanation (asteroid impact), while Text 2 systematically tests two alternative explanations (carbonate dissolution and subsurface salt withdrawal), ruling both out based on Nadir's central uplift. Text 1 presents supporting evidence; Text 2 eliminates competing hypotheses. A invents tone shifts that aren't there, B reverses what Text 1 discusses, and D falsely claims Text 2 refutes impact when it only tests alternatives. That matches choice C — C is correct.
